names.title: Falsehoods About Names
names.hook: Your name model is wrong, and it's wrong in ways that hurt real people.

sources.label: Falsehoods about Names — With Examples (Shine Solutions)
Mr. Null — My Name Makes Me Invisible to Computers (WIRED)

postal-addresses.title: Falsehoods About Postal Addresses
postal-addresses.hook: An address isn't a string, isn't a location, and sometimes isn't even necessary.

sources.label: Parsing the Infamous Japanese Postal CSV (dampfkraft)
Why doesn't Costa Rica use real addresses?

dates-and-time.title: Falsehoods About Dates and Time
dates-and-time.hook: Time isn't a line, a time zone isn't an offset, and the clock can run backwards.

sources.label: Falsehoods about Time and Time Zones (Creative Deletion)

emails.title: Falsehoods About Emails
emails.hook: Your email-validation regex is wrong, and the RFC is far weirder than you think.

sources.label: I Knew How to Validate an Email Until I Read the RFC (Haacked)
Your E-Mail Validation Logic is Wrong (netmeister)

phone-numbers.title: Falsehoods About Phone Numbers
phone-numbers.hook: A phone number isn't a number, isn't unique, and won't hold still.

geography.title: Falsehoods About Geography
geography.hook: Places don't have one name, borders move, and coordinates lie.

text-and-unicode.title: Falsehoods About Text and Unicode
text-and-unicode.hook: Unicode is not a character set, a character is not a code point, and string length is a lie.

sources.label: The Absolute Minimum About Unicode (Joel Spolsky)
Let's Stop Ascribing Meaning to Code Points (Manish Goregaokar)

networks.title: Falsehoods About Networks
networks.hook: The network is not reliable, an IP address has many spellings, and DNS is not what you think.

sources.label: There's more than one way to write an IP address (ma.ttias.be)

transportation.title: Falsehoods About Transportation
transportation.hook: Aviation data is messier than the planes, and a seat map is not a grid.

typography.title: Falsehoods About Typography
typography.hook: Fonts lie about their metrics, and case is not a simple toggle.

sources.label: Truths programmers should know about case (James Bennett)

web.title: Falsehoods About the Web
web.hook: HTML, URLs, and REST are all weirder than the spec lets on.

sources.label: The Hidden Complexity of Downloading Favicons (simplecto)

multimedia.title: Falsehoods About Multimedia
multimedia.hook: Video and music metadata break every clean assumption you bring to them.

sources.label: Horrible edge cases when dealing with music (dustri)

systems-of-measurement.title: Falsehoods About Systems of Measurement
systems-of-measurement.hook: Units don't convert as cleanly as grade-school math promised.

sources.label: Falsehoods about Systems of Measurement (Steve Moser)

computer-science.title: Falsehoods About Computer Science
computer-science.hook: The tidy abstractions you were taught leak everywhere in practice.

business-and-money.title: Falsehoods About Business and Money
business-and-money.hook: Money, prices, and economics break software in expensive ways.

cryptocurrency.title: Falsehoods About Cryptocurrency
cryptocurrency.hook: Crypto's clean abstractions hide jagged, irreversible edges.

job-applicants.title: Falsehoods About Job Applicants
job-applicants.hook: Your assumptions about applicants and their histories are mostly wrong.

game-development.title: Falsehoods About Game Development
game-development.hook: Even a single door is a bottomless pit of design decisions.

art.title: Falsehoods About Art
art.hook: Art objects refuse every database schema you'd design for them.

floating-point.title: Falsehoods About Floating Point
floating-point.hook: 0.1 + 0.2 ≠ 0.3, and that's the least of your problems.

file-paths.title: Falsehoods About File Paths
file-paths.hook: A path is not a string, and Windows paths are a different animal entirely.

sources.label: The weird world of Windows file paths (Fileside)

csvs.title: Falsehoods About CSVs
csvs.hook: CSV has a spec, and almost nothing in the wild obeys it.

null-pointers.title: Falsehoods About Null Pointers
null-pointers.hook: Null pointers are more cursed than pointers, and pointers are already cursed.

undefined-behavior.title: Falsehoods About Undefined Behavior
undefined-behavior.hook: Undefined behavior can cause literally anything — for a broader 'anything' than you imagine.

cpu-caches.title: Falsehoods About CPU Caches
cpu-caches.hook: What you believe about CPU caches is quietly breaking your concurrency.

yaml.title: Falsehoods About YAML
yaml.hook: YAML will turn your config into numbers, booleans, and the country of Norway.

sources.label: The YAML document from hell (Ruud van Asseldonk)

versions.title: Falsehoods About Software Versions
versions.hook: A version number is not a number, and it isn't ordered the way you think.

pagination.title: Falsehoods About Pagination
pagination.hook: Page 2 is not simply the rows after page 1.

cve.title: Falsehoods About CVEs
cve.hook: A CVE is not a vulnerability, and 36 other confusions.

garbage-collection.title: Falsehoods About Garbage Collection
garbage-collection.hook: GC doesn't mean you can't leak, and it isn't unpredictable magic.

urandom.title: Falsehoods About /dev/urandom
urandom.hook: Everything everyone repeats about /dev/urandom vs /dev/random is wrong.

event-driven-systems.title: Falsehoods About Event-Driven Systems
event-driven-systems.hook: Messages arrive once, in order, exactly as sent — and other comforting lies.

ai.title: Falsehoods About AI
ai.hook: What's true about AI today is a screenshot, not a law — cost, capability, and behavior all shifted while you read this.

sources.label: Falsehoods Vibe Coders Believe About LLMs (Wilson Hobbs)
Beyond the Hype: 10 Common Misconceptions About LLMs (Springer Nature)

file-systems.title: Falsehoods About File Systems
file-systems.hook: Writing a file looks atomic. It isn't — and your data is one power-cut away from finding out.

sources.label: Files are fraught with peril (Dan Luu)

localization.title: Falsehoods About Localization
localization.hook: Translating the strings is the easy 10%. Plurals, dates, money, and word order are the other 90%.

sources.label: Localization Failure: Temperature is Hard (Bruce Dawson)
